summary: Iteration 5 retest of the 3 previously failing portfolio target/stop-loss tests after main agent's fix in /api/alerts/sync (and _alerts_loop) — _check_target_crosses now runs BEFORE _check_price_moves so prev_price snapshot is intact when target detection executes. ALL 3 previously failing tests now PASS: test_target_hit_alert, test_stop_loss_hit_alert, test_target_alert_cooldown. Full regression: 52/52 PASS (24 portfolio + 12 alerts + 16 backend_test). No new failures. The order-swap fix is correct and targeted; no other side effects detected.

minor.issue: Response echoes only the fields supplied; consider returning merged target doc.
No ticker resolvability validation (unlike /api/watchlist which calls fetch_quote).

Function does not itself update ticker_state.last_price after firing — relies on _check_price_moves running afterwards. Current ordering (target first, price second) preserves correctness, but the implicit coupling remains fragile.

critical_code_review_comments: Fix is minimal and correct (server.py L549-550 swap). Consider adding an inline comment explaining the ordering invariant so future refactors don't accidentally re-introduce the bug.

Better long-term refactor: merge _check_target_crosses + _check_price_moves into one per-ticker pass that explicitly captures prev_price once and uses it for both detections, eliminating the ordering dependency entirely.
Lots stored with buy_date as string (YYYY-MM-DD) — sort works lexicographically; consider datetime for robustness.
POST /api/portfolio/lots accepts any regex-valid ticker without verifying it resolves at the data provider.
seed_data_creation: test_portfolio.py seeds position_lots, position_targets, ticker_state via pymongo. test_zz_final_cleanup drops position_lots, position_targets, alerts, ticker_state. Watchlist 12-default seed remains intact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 4 critical ordering bug is RESOLVED. /api/alerts/sync now calls _check_target_crosses before _check_price_moves; same fix applied in _alerts_loop background task. All 52 backend tests pass. If main agent makes further changes to alerts logic, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_portfolio.py — particularly test_target_hit_alert, test_stop_loss_hit_alert, test_target_alert_cooldown which directly verify the cross-detection invariant.
rca of the issue: Fix verified: server.py /api/alerts/sync now runs _check_target_crosses first (capturing prev_price from ticker_state before _check_price_moves overwrites it), then _check_price_moves advances last_price. Same ordering applied in _alerts_loop. Tests confirm: target_hits>=1 when last_price seeded below target with current quote above; stop_loss_hits>=1 for symmetric downward cross; second consecutive sync does NOT duplicate target_hit alert (6h cooldown honored).
